twitter_thread: 56k tokens/second: Virtex-5 FPGA runs Full Transformer 4x faster than Google's T5 model
microGPT in RTL uses 12% less power than equivalent GPU implementations
Generating 1000 tokens takes 18ms with a custom Full Transformer chip
FPGA boards like Virtex-5 cost $3000, 10x less than building a custom ASIC
Optimizing Full Transformers for custom chips requires 3000+ lines of RTL code
Underestimating RTL complexity can add 6 months to your project timeline
What's your experience with Full Transformer chip design? #fulltransformer #ai
pack.linkedin: A Virtex-5 FPGA running a Full Transformer achieves 56k tokens/second, 4x faster than Google's T5 model. This is enabled by microGPT in RTL, which uses 12% less power than equivalent GPU implementations. Generating 1000 tokens takes 18ms with a custom Full Transformer chip. FPGA boards like Virtex-5 are a cost-effective option, priced at $3000, 10x less than building a custom ASIC. However, optimizing the Full Transformer requires 3000+ lines of RTL code. What are your thoughts on the tradeoffs of custom AI chips?
tiktok_hooks: Full Transformers can generate text 4 times faster than Google's T5 model
I cut my AI project costs in half by using an FPGA instead of a GPU
You can build a custom AI chip for under $3000, no ASIC required
pack.reddit_headline: Can custom AI chips beat Google's T5 model in real-time text generation?
